libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: When generating tests that exercise OGNL reflective access against JDK classes, configure the test JVM with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` and `--add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED`. Do not enable or depend on installing a Java SecurityManager on JDK 25; avoid tests that call `System.setSecurityManager`, and keep `ognl.security.manager` unset unless explicitly testing OGNL's disabled/force-disabled initialization behavior.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: JDK 25 permanently disables SecurityManager support, so older OGNL security-manager tests can fail independently of reachability metadata.
OGNL's expression compiler uses Javassist at runtime; it is present transitively, but native-image validation remains authoritative for whether compiled-expression paths are usable.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL is a pure Java expression/reflection library and its only runtime dependency, Javassist, is already transitive from ognl:ognl:3.3.5, so no extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed. However upstream OGNL 3.3.5 configures Java 9+ test runs with module opens, so meaningful reflective coverage may need JVM args beyond the normal scaffold.
